quiz.title: Unit 5 Quiz 4, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Polyatomic Ions: Naming and Formulas
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Polyatomic Ions, Naming Ionic Compounds with Polyatomic Ions, Formula Writing, Oxyanions, Common Polyatomic Ions

questions.question: Name K₂CO₃:
questions.explanation: K₂CO₃ is named potassium carbonate. It contains the carbonate ion (CO₃²⁻). Ionic compounds do not use prefixes, so dipotassium carbonate (C) is incorrect. Potassium carbonite (B) would be K₂CO₂, and potassium oxide (D) is K₂O.

questions.question: Which is the formula for calcium nitrate?
questions.explanation: Calcium nitrate is Ca(NO₃)₂. Calcium has a +2 charge (Ca²⁺) and nitrate is NO₃⁻ with a -1 charge, so two nitrates are needed. Ca(NO₂)₂ (D) would be calcium nitrite.

questions.question: Name Mg₃(PO₄)₂:
questions.explanation: Mg₃(PO₄)₂ is named magnesium phosphate. It contains the phosphate ion (PO₄³⁻). Magnesium only forms one type of ion (+2), so no Roman numeral is needed. Magnesium phosphite (B) would contain PO₃³⁻.

questions.question: Name NH₄NO₃:
questions.explanation: NH₄NO₃ is named ammonium nitrate. It contains two polyatomic ions: ammonium (NH₄⁺) and nitrate (NO₃⁻). Ammonia nitrate (C) is incorrect because ammonia is NH₃, not NH₄⁺.

questions.question: Which formula is magnesium hydroxide?
questions.explanation: Magnesium hydroxide is Mg(OH)₂. Magnesium has a +2 charge (Mg²⁺) and hydroxide is OH⁻ with a -1 charge, so two hydroxides are needed. MgO (D) is magnesium oxide.

questions.question: Name Ba₃N₂:
questions.explanation: Ba₃N₂ is named barium nitride. It is an ionic compound containing the nitride ion (N³⁻). Barium only forms one type of ion (+2), so no Roman numeral is needed, and prefixes are not used for ionic compounds.

questions.question: Name CO:
questions.explanation: CO is named carbon monoxide. It is a covalent compound, so prefixes are used. The 'mono-' prefix indicates one oxygen atom. Carbon dioxide (C) would be CO₂. Carbon (II) oxide (D) is an older, less common name.

questions.question: Name KClO₃:
questions.explanation: KClO₃ is named potassium chlorate. It contains the chlorate ion (ClO₃⁻). Potassium chlorite (A) would be KClO₂, potassium hypochlorite (C) would be KClO, and potassium perchlorate (D) would be KClO₄.

questions.question: What is the name of CO₃²⁻?
questions.explanation: CO₃²⁻ is named carbonate. It is a polyatomic ion with a 2- charge. Carbonite (A) would be CO₂²⁻ (which is not a common ion). Carboxylate (C) refers to a functional group in organic chemistry (RCOO⁻). Carbon oxide (D) would refer to neutral compounds like CO or CO₂.

questions.question: NH₃ is known as:
questions.explanation: NH₃ is commonly known as ammonia. It is a covalent compound with the systematic name nitrogen trihydride (C), but the common name ammonia is widely accepted and used. Ammonium (A) is NH₄⁺, the polyatomic cation. Nitrous acid (D) is HNO₂.

questions.question: NH₄⁺ is known as:
questions.explanation: NH₄⁺ is known as ammonium. It is a polyatomic cation with a +1 charge. Ammonia (A) is NH₃, the neutral molecule. Nitrogen tetrahydride (C) is not a standard name for this ion. Amide (D) is NH₂⁻ or refers to organic compounds with -CONH₂ groups.

questions.question: Name HBrO (oxyacid):
questions.explanation: HBrO is named hypobromous acid. The pattern for bromine oxyacids follows the anion names: BrO⁻ (hypobromite) → hypobromous acid, BrO₂⁻ (bromite) → bromous acid, BrO₃⁻ (bromate) → bromic acid. Hydrobromic acid (A) would be HBr (binary acid).

questions.question: Which of the following is a polyatomic ion?
questions.explanation: SO₄²⁻ (sulfate) is a polyatomic ion — a charged species composed of two or more atoms covalently bonded together. Na⁺, Cl⁻, and Mg²⁺ are all monatomic ions (single atoms with a charge).

questions.question: What is the name of NO₃⁻?
questions.explanation: NO₃⁻ is named nitrate. Nitrite (A) is NO₂⁻. Nitride (C) is N³⁻. Nitrogen oxide (D) would refer to neutral compounds like NO or NO₂.

questions.question: What is the correct formula for sodium nitrate?
questions.explanation: Sodium nitrate is NaNO₃. Sodium has a +1 charge (Na⁺) and nitrate is NO₃⁻ with a -1 charge, so they combine in a 1:1 ratio. NaNO₂ (A) would be sodium nitrite, and Na₃N (D) would be sodium nitride.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered naming and formulas with polyatomic ions! 🧪📝
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review common polyatomic ions and their charges.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on distinguishing between -ate and -ite endings.
scoreMessages.0: Please review polyatomic ion names, formulas, and how to combine them with metals to form ionic compounds.
